items.title: Couple forced to live on verandah for three months awaiting flood repairs

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: Legal aid worker caught in bed with fugitive teen killer pleads guilty

children.content: A 37-year-old legal aid worker who was arrested while sharing a hotel bed with a teenage killer she helped evade authorities after he breached his parole has pleaded guilty to attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A tracking bracelet on a man's ankle.

items.title: Men tear gassed in Darwin's youth prison have payout slashed by High Court

children.content: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

gallery.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

items.title: NT's new climate resilience plan lists gas project as top priority

children.content: The Northern Territory government's newly released climate resilience plan has listed the acceleration of gas extraction at the Beetaloo Basin as a top priority. Environment groups have fiercely criticised the plan, saying it will "make climate change worse".

image.altText: An aerial view of a fracking exploration site in the Beetaloo Basin.

items.title: Unions and Inpex reach deal after weeks of strikes and negotiation

children.content: Workers at the Northern Territory's Inpex Ichthys facility have restarted regular duties after unions and the Japanese-owned company struck a deal to end a dispute that threatened to disrupt LNG exports.

image.altText: Inpex's Ichthys LNG Plant is on Darwin Harbour.

items.title: Women forced to labour in tearoom, run-down wards at NT's busiest hospital

children.content: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, holding a baby who is facing away while she looks right at the camera.

items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

image.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands's $12m settlement won't deter Jackie O's legal battle

children.content: A legal battle between Jackie "O" Henderson and ARN Media appears still headed for an October trial after her former co-host Kyle Sandilands settled his own court case against the company.

image.altText: A selfie of Jackie O Henderson smiling with Kyle Sandilands in the background wearing radio headphones.

items.title: England's high-octane start betrays World Cup nerves

children.content: England's World Cup opener against Croatia, with a missed penalty and a flaky defence, seemed to be briefly polluted by fear.

image.altText: Harry Kane claps his hands

items.title: 'I'm blaming JD': Key moments from Trump's extraordinary Iran deal press conference

children.content: Donald Trump has held a press conference as the United States and Tehran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the war in Iran.

articles.title: Trump earns laughs as hot mics at G7 summit capture leaders discussing lighter topics

children.content: As world leaders made their way into conference rooms at a lakeside resort in Évian-les-Bains, microphones caught plenty of off-the-cuff banter.

image.altText: Donald Trump looks on as he is spoken to by emmanuel macron

articles.title: US-Iran deal covers Lebanon, Strait of Hormuz and Iran's uranium

children.content: The presidents of the United States and Iran have officially signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: A man in a suit talking and gesturing with his hand, seen from close up.

items.title: Coroner suggests racism marred investigation of Indigenous teen's death

children.content: The mysterious death of Mark Haines, whose body was discovered on railway tracks in northern NSW in 1988, would have been treated differently "if it was a white boy", according to the coroner.

image.altText: A composite image of Mark Haines with railway tracks.

items.title: Key concessions in ICAC's 'inquisition' of Pink Ladies founding member

children.content: The former Parramatta council CEO described the public hearings into her alleged corruption as "the Spanish Inquisition."

image.altText: Gail wears a pale blazer and striped shirt and dark glasses. She walks alongside two men in suits.

items.title: Homeless woman feels safer living in vehicle than shared housing

children.content: Housing advocates say an increasing number of single, older women are choosing to live in vehicles while they wait for social housing.

image.altText: woman holding two dogs sitting on stairs of motorhome

items.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood is dead

children.content: The tree is said to have sheltered Robin Hood, the legendary 13th-century bandit who stole from the rich and gave to the poor and took refuge in the Nottingham forest.

image.altText: An upwards view of tree trunks.

items.title: 'I forgive him': Heartbroken parents speak after children in pram killed in crash

children.content: The heartbroken parents of two children killed when they were hit by a car in Sydney's south-west yesterday have spoken of their shock, saying they forgive the driver.

gallery.altText: Woman in distress  wearing black and white jumper surrounded by teary women

thumbnail.altText: Woman in distress  wearing black and white jumper surrounded by teary women

items.title: ASX down, oil falls as US-Iran peace deal lifts outlook for supply

children.content: The ASX is down today, led by mining and tech stocks. Oil has also fallen as the US-Iran Peace deal lifts outlook for supply. Follow live.

image.altText: An electronic billboard showing stock prices is seen in a window reflection.

items.title: After 27 years, Virginia Trioli takes creative leap and leaves the ABC

children.content: After 27 years as a journalist, broadcaster and program maker at the ABC, Virginia Trioli announces she is leaving to pursue new creative opportunities.

image.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

items.title: Sell-out shows, cult status line-up: How Dark Mofo catapults artists

children.content: Her headline show at Dark Mofo sold out in just over an hour, something Tasmanian Indigenous singer Miss Kaninna says couldn't have happened without the winter festival's ability to attract "massive, massive" music industry names.

gallery.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.
Emi Doi stands on stage with two dancers and holds a microphone.
People stand around an open fire at night.

thumbnail.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.
Emi Doi stands on stage with two dancers and holds a microphone.
People stand around an open fire at night.

items.title: Quiz: Grab your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which airport hosts the world's largest indoor waterfall? What is the Swiss delicacy raclette?

image.altText: Brisbane cityscape and river in March 2021.

items.title: Sarah Game won't rule out introducing another bill to restrict late term abortion

children.content: Family First upper house MLC Sarah Game has not ruled out introducing another bill restricting late-term abortion access, after her proposed legislation to restrict abortion after 25 weeks failed in the lower house last night.

image.altText: A woman in a black dress sits on a red plush couch with wooden desks in front of her.

items.title: Locals fear massive data centre will use water from 'stressed' River Murray

children.content: A massive data centre proposed for SA faces scrutiny over water and environmental risks.

gallery.altText: A man with a handle bar moustache poses on farmland.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a handle bar moustache poses on farmland.

items.title: Snowtown serial killer's release subject to another review

children.content: Another review will be held to decide whether Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis should be released on parole.

image.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

items.title: Couple left with nothing after investing $1m in 7-Eleven franchise

children.content: Jotika and Sunny Sharma took out a loan to purchase their Sydney franchise in 2015. When the lease expired this year, their relationship with the convenience store giant turned ugly.

image.altText: Couple look at the camera, both with feelings of distress on their faces.

items.title: Walker's breakout Origin performance showed us both sides of his coin

children.content: On Wednesday night, Queensland coach Billy Slater got a glimpse of a glistening Sam Walker-led future, even if the present wasn't quite perfect.

articles.title: How Max Plath became Queensland's newest Origin cult hero

children.content: The players who fill the spaces in between the stars are the secret to Queensland's Origin dominance and in Max Plath the Maroons have found yet another player who knows just how to do it.

image.altText: Queensland sing the national anthem before an Origin game

articles.title: Maroons star reveals dad's message before heartbreaking death

children.content: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui shares the advice his dad gave him before his death after a long battle with cancer.

image.altText: A man breaks through a tackle during a football game.

articles.title: There's Blue blood in the water after NSW collapse in Origin II

children.content: New South Wales had travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp, but after last night's disaster, success has never felt further away.

image.altText: A group of players commiserate after an Origin match

gallery.altText: Sam Walker and Harry Grant celebrate with Lindsay Collins after a State of Origin try.

thumbnail.altText: Sam Walker and Harry Grant celebrate with Lindsay Collins after a State of Origin try.

items.title: Hantavirus ship passenger recounts 'huge shock' as quarantine nears end

children.content: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.

image.altText: An elderly grey haired man.

items.title: Global hacking group targets North Queensland cane producer

children.content: A Russian-speaking ransomware operation known as The Gentlemen has reportedly claimed responsibility on the dark web for a cyber attack that shut down two regional Queensland sugar mills for a week.

image.altText: A graphic of a sugar mill with the silhouette of a man wearing a top hat.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Star whacks US pundit and Mabil's heartwarming moment

children.content: Zlatan Ibrahimovic drops "ignorant" bomb to Fox Sports colleagues, Scotland's fans almost drink Boston dry and Awer Mabil gets starstruck in front of SBS legend David Basheer. Here are the quick hits from today at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: Colombia beats Uzbekistan to wrap up first round of group games

children.content: Every team has now played a game at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Ghana scoring a last-gasp winner, England taking down Croatia, Colombia beating the Uzbeks, and Portugal held by DR Congo.

image.altText: Daniel Muñoz stretches for the ball

articles.title: From refugee to Socceroos star: The grassroots club that gave Irankunda a go

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

image.altText: A young boy in a soccer uniform is running and kicking a ball.

gallery.altText: A collation of a picture of three men.

thumbnail.altText: A collation of a picture of three men.

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

items.title: Uni students tackle 'character-building' remote living through placements

children.content: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

image.altText: A small tent and camping chair.

items.title: Drone footage reveals extent of seal deaths from bird flu on Australian island

children.content: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

items.title: HSBC agrees to pay $35 million penalty after widespread scam failures

children.content: HSBC has admitted to failing to protect customers from scams and agreed to a $35 million penalty in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Low shot of the HSBC building in Sydney with the logo on large glass panels.

items.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time

children.content: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

image.altText: Woman with head in hands with tax return on screen in the background.

items.title: Weaponising homophobia, this Aussie horror is terrifying

children.content: Writer and director Adrian Chiarella's debut supernatural horror feature, Leviticus, is as romantic as it is scary.

gallery.altText: A young man holds another young man by the head

thumbnail.altText: A young man holds another young man by the head
